sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 54-year-old male, presents with a 2-3 month history of stabbing, shooting pain in the right cheek bone area of his face.
The pain is exacerbated by certain temperatures and wind, and is described as excruciating.
The patient reports that the pain has not been relieved by ibuprofen, Motrin, or tiger balm.
He has had episodes of pain lasting from a few minutes to up to an hour, with the pain usually subsiding after a few minutes.
He denies numbness or tingling in the affected area and reports no weakness on the right side of his face.
Past medical history: The patient denies a history of multiple sclerosis or brain tumors.
He reports occasional headaches, but no history of migraines.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms in other organs that are relevant to his initial presentation.
Current medications: The patient is currently taking Tylenol for occasional headaches.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's smile appears symmetric, and he reports being able to hear equally on both sides when asked to show his teeth and hear a sound.
The patient reports a little bit of numbness on the right side of his face when a cotton tape is run along the side of his face.
The patient reports that the left side feels sharp when a needle is poked, while the right side feels dull.
Test Results: The patient's MRI shows a small blood vessel abutting and potentially pinching the trigeminal nerve.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a condition called trigeminal neuralgia, where the compression of the trigeminal nerve by a small blood vessel causes shooting, electrical pain in the face.
Problem list: Trigeminal neuralgia; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Tegretol 100mg, to be taken as needed for pain control.
I will have the patient follow up in one month to assess the efficacy of the medication.
If the patient's pain continues, we will discuss surgical options to treat the compression of the trigeminal nerve.
The patient will also undergo genetic testing to ensure the safety of the medication.
Follow-up: The patient will return in one month to assess the efficacy of the medication and discuss further treatment options if necessary.
